methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing sectioned shells using laser ablation coupled with inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) to measure isotopic compositions. The method is used to obtain precise isotopic data from shell samples.

steps.description: The sectioned shells were sampled using a New Wave Research 213 nm laser ablation system, coupled to a Thermo X Series2 Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometer (LA-ICP-MS) at Cardiff University using the parameters shown in Table 2.
steps.searchable_summary: sample shells using LA-ICP-MS with specified parameters

steps.description: The sample was moved under the laser at 11 μm/s. At higher speeds, fragments of shell were seen to break off, causing undesired spikes in signal intensity on ionisation. At slower laser speeds, signal intensity was attenuated as less material escaped the deeper laser ablation pits.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize laser speed to prevent signal spikes and attenuation

steps.description: Laser tracks were run as close to the inside of the shell as possible to stay within the secondary layer for T. retusa, and within the tertiary layer for L. neozelanica.
steps.searchable_summary: align laser tracks to specific shell layers

steps.description: 24 Mg, 25 Mg, 27 Al, 43 Ca, 44 Ca, 48 Ca, 55 Mn, 57 Fe, 84 Sr, 86 Sr and 88 Sr peaks were averaged by the mass spectrometer every 0.5 s.
NIST 610 glass was analysed as a standard between each sample track.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze NIST 610 glass as a standard

steps.description: Each individual data point represents an averaged signal from material ablated by a 40 μm laser spot that moved 5.5 μm along the laser track; the spatial resolution is therefore ~50 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate spatial resolution from laser spot and track movement

steps.description: Due to a 5 s time delay between firing the laser and obtaining a stable signal, adjoining tracks were overlapped by 60 μm to ensure continuous data profiles across the shell.
steps.searchable_summary: overlap laser tracks to ensure continuous data profiles

methods.description: This method involves drilling powder samples from shell trenches for stable isotope analysis, which is used to determine isotopic compositions in shells.

steps.description: Powder for stable isotope analyses was drilled using a New Wave Research Micromill after acquisition of LA-ICP-MS data, in the same location as the ablated tracks.
steps.searchable_summary: drill powder samples for stable isotope analysis

steps.description: Trenches were drilled as close to the inner margin of the shell as possible.
steps.searchable_summary: drill trenches near inner shell margin

steps.description: After each trench was drilled and the powdered sample transferred to a glass vial, the trench, sample surface and drill bit were cleaned with deionised water and a brush to ensure no cross contamination between trenches.
steps.searchable_summary: clean equipment to prevent cross contamination

steps.description: Samples were analysed at Cardiff University using an automated carbonate preparation device (Kiel-III) coupled online to a Thermo Finnigan MAT252 stable isotope mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using Kiel-III and MAT252

methods.description: This method involves screening laser ablation data to identify and remove contamination from the profiles, ensuring accurate representation of natural element incorporation in shells.

steps.description: High Mg/Ca and Sr/Ca signals were occasionally recorded at the valve margins, which resulted from the ablation of resin. These data were easily identified by their reduced 43 Ca signal intensity and removed from the profiles presented here.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and remove resin contamination from data

steps.description: Alongside Mg, Sr and Ca, the isotopes 27 Al, 55 Mn and 57 Fe were collected for the purpose of screening for contamination of the brachiopod calcite.
Data with Al/Ca N4 μmol/mol were therefore discarded from the L. neozelanica profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: discard data with high Al/Ca ratios
